steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Critics describe Anderson's film as 'galvanizing' and Hudlin's film as 'exuberant' — both are enthusiastically positive terms. The blank names what fans hope this critical praise will do for audiences.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'impartiality': means neutrality or lack of bias. Critics expressing strong enthusiasm are the opposite of impartial. ✗
  • Choice B — 'ambivalence': means mixed or conflicted feelings. These reviews are unambiguously positive, not mixed. ✗
  • Choice C — 'foresight': means the ability to predict the future. Critics are evaluating films, not forecasting anything. ✗
  • Choice D — 'acclaim': means enthusiastic public praise. 'Galvanizing' and 'exuberant' are exactly the language of critical acclaim, and fans hope this praise draws new viewers. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
